problems.title: The Training Phase
problems.desc: Training a foundational 70B parameter model consumes approximately 1,000 MWh of electricity over several months. This alone generates carbon emissions roughly equivalent to flying 300 passengers round-trip from New York to London.

problems.title: Inference at Scale
problems.desc: While training is a massive one-time cost, inference is eternal. Millions of users generating billions of tokens daily requires data centers to run massive GPU clusters 24/7, vastly surpassing training costs over a model's lifecycle.

problems.title: Cooling Overhead
problems.desc: For every watt of power used by a GPU to calculate matrix multiplications, another massive fraction is spent on liquid and air cooling systems to prevent server meltdown, doubling the actual grid footprint.

solutions.title: Semantic & KV Caching
solutions.desc: By hashing and storing the 'Key-Value' states of previous context, we avoid re-computing identical prompt prefixes. Implementing semantic caches layer prevents repeated generation of similar answers entirely.

solutions.title: Mixture of Experts
solutions.desc: Sparse models route inputs to specific 'expert' clusters. Instead of activating 100% of a 70B model, an MoE might only activate 14B parameters per token. This yields massive compute and VRAM bandwidth savings.

solutions.title: Sub-byte Quantization
solutions.desc: Shrinking precision from FP16 to INT4 or lower. Utilizing techniques like AWQ allows inference on hardware that consumes a fraction of the power without catastrophic degradation of reasoning capability.
